channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-06-01T11:49:07.584Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Per WHO Disease Outbreak News (29 May 2026), the DRC has reported 125 confirmed cases (17 deaths) and 906 suspected cases (223 suspected deaths, as of 27 May), and Uganda has reported 9 confirmed cases (1 death). Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (11 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,983 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 28), representing 30 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: Google News: Bangladesh records three more suspected measles deaths in 24 hours - newagebd.net
item.description: Bangladesh records three more suspected measles deaths in 24 hours newagebd.net

item.title: ABC News: Hundreds of youths protest outside Kenya's Ebola quarantine center for US citizens
item.description: Hundreds of youths in the Kenyan town of Nanyuki have demonstrated against an Ebola quarantine center at Laikipia Air Base

item.title: NBC News: New Pancreatic Cancer Drug Doubles Survival Rate in Trials
item.description: A new drug, decades in the making, is showing game-changing results for those battling pancreatic cancer. NBC’s medical contributor Dr. Natalie Azar joins TODAY with what you need to know about the breakthrough medication that doubled survival time and slowed tumor...

item.title: Google News: Hospitals in Brazil Test Two Patients With Ebola-Like Symptoms - TODAY.com
item.description: Hospitals in Brazil Test Two Patients With Ebola-Like Symptoms TODAY.com

item.title: Google News: Health body fast-tracks three Ebola vaccines as virus continues spread - Euronews.com
item.description: Health body fast-tracks three Ebola vaccines as virus continues spread Euronews.com

item.title: NBC News: Brazil investigates possible Ebola cases amid scramble to contain outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization’s top doctor has traveled to the Ebola hot zone to help bring the unfolding crisis under control as suspected cases of the especially deadly strain of the disease top 1,100

item.title: Google News: Brazil investigates possible Ebola cases amid scramble to contain outbreak - NBC News
item.description: Brazil investigates possible Ebola cases amid scramble to contain outbreak NBC News

item.title: NBC News: Hospitals in Brazil Test Two Patients With Ebola-Like Symptoms
item.description: The director general of the World Health Organization has arrived in the Democratic Republic of Congo as doctors on the ground race to play catch up against the third largest Ebola outbreak in history. In Rio De Janeiro and São Paulo, Brazil, hospitals are testing two...

item.title: Google News: Cholera kills 77 in West Kordofan as Sudan holds emergency health meeting - Sudan Tribune
item.description: Cholera kills 77 in West Kordofan as Sudan holds emergency health meeting Sudan Tribune

item.title: NPR: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: The outbreak remains focused in Congo's eastern Ituri province. Congo has reported over 1,000 suspected cases with the Bundibugyo virus, which has no approved treatment or vaccine.

item.title: Google News: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries - NPR
item.description: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries NPR

item.description: Trump focuses on keeping Ebola out of US over fighting outbreak abroad ABC27

item.description: Opinion | The U.S. needs a tough Ebola policy — before the virus gets here The Washington Post

item.title: Google News: ‘The Church is on the front line of the Ebola response’: Anglicans act to tackle Ebola outbreak - Anglican Communion News Service

item.title: ABC News: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo reach 282 as survivors describe their recoveries
item.description: Congolese authorities say there are now at least 282 confirmed cases of Ebola in its ongoing outbreak
